pattern: A custom sort key that ranks each element by its position in the reference order, with anything missing pushed to the end and broken by value, reproduces the desired arrangement.
hints: Elements that appear in the second array must come out in that array's order; everything else trails behind in ascending order.
Give each element a sort key: if it is in the reference list use its index there, otherwise a sentinel that sorts last together with its own value as a tiebreaker.
Build a map from value to its rank in arr2, then sort arr1 by (rank if present else a large constant, value).

edge_inputs.description: values not in arr2 sort ascending at the tail
all elements absent from arr2 just sort ascending
duplicates of a referenced value stay grouped in order
mix of present and absent with absent spanning low and high

stress.description: large arr1 over a small value domain with a partial reference list
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr1 = [rng.randint(0, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    present = sorted(set(rng.sample(range(0, 51), rng.randint(1, 20))))
    rng.shuffle(present)
    return [arr1, present]
stress.note: arr2 values are distinct as required and a subset of the value domain
